caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Supplied role mix is 5-digit 54192 and is shared with portrait studios; retail and process shares need not represent commercial studios.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions separate task automation from terminal demand; generative substitution is assessed in terminal value rather than used to inflate V.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The residual wage bucket likely contains production assistants and digital technicians whose mix differs by commercial subsegment.
inputs.pi_dist: No reopened source measures the mix of day rate, production fee and usage-license revenue for NAICS 541922.
inputs.pi_dist: Agency compensation evidence is a buyer-side proxy, not a commercial-studio contract census.
inputs.pi_moat: Copyright and consent constraints are not a general pricing moat and are not counted as terminal protection twice.
inputs.pi_moat: Client relationship ownership is target-specific and may sit with the individual photographer rather than the studio.
inputs.t50_years: Adobe is a global creator survey, Aftershoot samples photographers who already integrated AI, and Census is economy-wide; none is a NAICS 541922 effective-adoption census.
inputs.t50_years: The range can move A materially and crosses the Pass/Conditional neighborhood when combined with other uncertainty.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No direct U.S. commercial-photography firm survey was reopened.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Tool use, post-processing automation and firm-wide effective adoption are different constructs.
inputs.historical_analogs: No code-specific historical technology-adoption series was reopened.
inputs.historical_analogs: The analogue is directional and is not an independent forecast of revenue substitution.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Not an observed commercial-photography owner-age statistic; the input should not be read as demographic evidence.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of reopened documentation is not evidence that no succession issue exists.
inputs.active_consolidators: Not a census: undiscovered local buyers or adjacent media consolidators could exist.
inputs.active_consolidators: This input is used only in B and is not evidence for V or A.
inputs.buy_mult: BizBuySell's SDE basis includes owner economics and is not interchangeable with normalized EBITDA.
inputs.buy_mult: Communication and Media is broader than commercial photography and the reported transactions are generally smaller than the requested EBITDA band.
inputs.exit_mult: No direct commercial-photography EBITDA exit series was reopened.
inputs.exit_mult: A target with recurring contracts and management depth could differ materially, but that cannot be inferred from this industry record.
inputs.pricing_structure: No reopened NAICS 541922 pricing survey quantified the revenue mix.
inputs.pricing_structure: Usage licensing can preserve value for authentic capture while being especially exposed where a client accepts synthetic imagery.

cross_checks.terminal_value: Registered sources establish generative-image capability and authenticity constraints, but not the share of NAICS 541922 revenue exposed to synthetic substitution; MELTING remains a low-confidence directional judgment rather than measured demand evidence.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $197,654 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541922); class <5: 4,417 firms, avg revenue/firm $304,123 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $47,595 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 272 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,123,423 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $175,816 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 122 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,767,893 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $276,675 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 74 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,381,662 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $998,730 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 11 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,048,909 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $946,654 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 6 firms, avg revenue/firm $13,065,500 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,044,751 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 6

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Assistive AI materially automates editing, culling, file preparation and administration, but physical capture, creative direction and client-facing production remain partly human. Fractions measure technically automatable task share, not layoffs or demand substitution.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541922 ($721,209,516) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541922 ($2,481,739,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: All deterministic mechanics, identity, route, dataset, fresh-finalization, memo, digest, and arithmetic checks pass. All seven registered score-driving sources were reopened and provide useful directional support, but the evidence is mostly broader than NAICS 541922 and does not directly quantify several selected estimates. No material defect was found; the kill thesis remains publishable with the authored caveats and the added non-fatal terminal-demand evidence caveat.

sources.title: AI & Photography: A Field Guide for ASMP Members (2025)
sources.what_it_supports: ASMP describes generative image creation, assistive editing uses, client disclosure and licensing practices, and the continuing importance of substantial human authorship.

sources.title: Adobe Creators' Toolkit Report announcement
sources.what_it_supports: Adobe reports a global survey of more than 16,000 creators in which 86% actively used creative generative AI; editing, enhancement and new asset generation were leading uses.

sources.title: 4As State of GenAI Adoption 2025
sources.what_it_supports: The advertising-agency survey reports 75% of respondents using GenAI, production efficiency expectations, and compensation-model pressure from time-saving AI.

sources.title: The Microstructure of AI Diffusion: Evidence from Firms, Business Functions, and Worker Tasks
sources.what_it_supports: Census BTOS research reports 18% firm AI use in a business function in late 2025/early 2026, higher use in professional services, and limited breadth of use among adopters.

sources.title: The Great Photography Workflow Shift Revolution
sources.what_it_supports: Aftershoot's 2025 survey of photographers already using AI reports heavy post-processing automation and expectations of further post-production automation.

sources.title: Copyright Office Releases Part 2 of Artificial Intelligence Report
sources.what_it_supports: The U.S. Copyright Office says AI outputs can be protected only where a human author determines sufficient expressive elements, while AI assistance does not itself bar copyrightability.

sources.title: Business Valuation Multiples by Industry
sources.what_it_supports: BizBuySell reports trailing-five-year Main Street transaction benchmarks, explains that reported earnings multiples are seller's discretionary earnings, and reports a 2.47x Communication and Media sector earnings multiple.

title: Commercial Photography
